scope_note: QA Diadic Inner Structure cert [317]. Primary sources: Iverson, B. (1975-1996) QA-1 pp.2-3,53-56 (open investigation); Chace (1927) Rhind Mathematical Papyrus MAA; Gillings (1972) MIT Press ISBN 978-0-486-24315-3. Discovery: computational continuation of cert [316] open frontier. CLAIM: (C1) Generator formula n=2*lcm(c1,c2,c3)-sum(lcm//ci) holds for all 7 standard 4-term Rhind entries {29,43,61,73,79,83,89}, each verified as exact Fraction. (C2) BEDA(2,1,3,4) is the female of BEDA(1,1,2,3) (Fibonacci seed) via female transform (b,e,d,a)->(2e,b,a,2d); its 7-element set {b,e,d,a,C,F,G}={2,1,3,4,6,8,10} contains the inner set for 5 of 8 four-term Rhind entries as 3-element subsets: n=29 inner={b,C,F}={2,6,8}, n=61 inner={a,F,G}={4,8,10}, n=79 inner={d,a,G}={3,4,10}, n=89 inner={a,C,G}={4,6,10}, n=101 inner={b,d,C}={2,3,6} (degenerate). (C3) Remaining 3: male (3,2,5,7) gives n=43 inner {b,e,a}={2,3,7}; female (4,3,7,10) of (3,2,5,7) gives n=79 inner {b,e,a}={3,4,10}; BEDA(1,1,2,3) inner {a,C,G}={3,4,5} and BEDA(3,1,4,5) inner {b,d,a}={3,4,5} both give n=73; female (4,1,5,6) of male (1,2,3,5) gives n=83 inner {b,d,a}={4,5,6}. (C4) n=101: inner sum=1, the unique 3-distinct-unit-fraction representation of 1 is 1/2+1/3+1/6; p=n=101 (prime), degenerate (generator formula inapplicable). (C5) Theorem NT: scribal subset choice is observer; BEDA/Pythagorean elements, generator formula, and female transform are discrete QA claims. Builds on cert [316] (Double Quantum Number, general 3-term formula).
